display.purpose: Atmospheric page titles and the home Japanese wordmark.

body.purpose: Readable tool copy, descriptions, and explanatory text.

label.purpose: Metadata, signal labels, footer links, and compact controls.

shadows.name: panel-night-shadow
shadows.purpose: Selected star panels and rare floating overlays.

shadows.name: text-grounding-shadow
shadows.purpose: Keeping display text legible over moving stars.

motion.name: atlas-ease-out
motion.purpose: Single-stage camera focus and restore motion.

components.name: Atlas Primary Button
components.description: Compact starlight action for opening mapped objects.
components.css: .ds-atlas-button { background: #f8fafc; color: #02040a; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15); border-radius: 2px; padding: 7px 12px; font: 600 11px/1.2 system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; transition: background 180ms ease-out, border-color 180ms ease-out; } .ds-atlas-button:hover { background: #ffffff; border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.35); } .ds-atlas-button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(170,215,220,0.55); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Star Detail Panel
components.description: Mapped-object panel attached to a selected star.
components.html:
SIGNAL 01

METEO TIMER

A quiet tool filed into the night sky.
components.css: .ds-star-panel { width: 280px; background: rgba(5,7,13,0.76); color: #f8fafc; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.14); border-radius: 2px; padding: 12px 16px; box-shadow: 0 22px 70px rgba(0,0,0,0.54), 0 0 34px rgba(170,215,220,0.12); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); } .ds-star-panel h3 { margin: 4px 0 12px; font: 600 14px/1.2 system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-star-panel p { margin: 0; color: rgba(248,250,252,0.7); font: 400 12px/1.6 system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; } .ds-panel-label { color: rgba(248,250,252,0.42); font: 600 10px/1.2 system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.22em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Atlas Link
components.css: .ds-atlas-link { color: rgba(248,250,252,0.48); font: 600 11px/1.2 system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; text-decoration: none; transition: color 180ms ease-out; } .ds-atlas-link:hover { color: rgba(248,250,252,0.88); } .ds-atlas-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(170,215,220,0.55); outline-offset: 4px; }

narrative.overview: Hoshikuzu should feel like a private astronomical index: precise, dimly lit, and intentional. The interface is not a spacecraft dashboard and not a marketing page. It is a working atlas where each 2D element annotates the sky and each tool feels like an instrument filed into the same collection.
keyCharacteristics: Deep-space canvas with warm off-white text.
Small uppercase metadata labels with generous tracking.
Thin rules, tick marks, and pointer lines used as annotation, not decoration.
Low-opacity panels that preserve the starfield as the primary atmosphere.
Single-stage motion with ease-out timing and no dramatic choreography.

rules.name: The Stars Own The Light Rule
rules.body: Page-wide gradients, decorative halos, and fixed nebula images are prohibited. Light should come from stars, focus states, and functional annotations.

rules.name: The Ten Percent Accent Rule
rules.body: Cyan and amber accents are rare signals. If an accent is visible before the content is understood, it is too loud.

rules.name: The Index Voice Rule
rules.body: Labels may be uppercase and tracked; body copy must remain calm and readable. Do not turn every word into a label.

rules.name: The Flat Until Focused Rule
rules.body: Surfaces should not float by default. Use elevation only when the user has selected, hovered, opened, or focused something.

dos: Do make 2D UI feel like annotations, labels, and instruments attached to a star atlas.
Do keep accents rare and meaningful, especially pale-cyan and soft-amber.
Do use thin hairlines, compact metadata, and carefully aligned panels to add design presence.
Do keep motion single-stage, short, and physically plausible.
Do preserve readability over the starfield with local darkening, text shadows, or restrained panel fills.
donts: Don't make it feel like a generic SaaS landing page with oversized hero metrics, repeated feature cards, and conversion copy.
Don't make it feel like a neon sci-fi HUD, cyberpunk control panel, or game menu.
Don't use heavy glassmorphism, bokeh blobs, purple-blue gradient wash, or decorative glow as the default answer.
Don't use real nebula imagery as a fixed screen background. It reads as a foreground sticker when the 3D scene rotates.
Don't let 2D UI compete with the starfield. The interface should annotate the sky, not cover it.
